title: Today's AI Brief: Data Centers and Disparities
summary: A look at the ongoing data center debates and infrastructure developments.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI brief. Today, I'm looking at some fresh signals from the world of artificial intelligence and data centers. There's quite a bit happening, from new infrastructure projects to community pushback. It's a complex space, and I'm here to give you the lowdown.

First up, the mayor of Shelbyville, Indiana, Scott Furgeson, has stirred up some controversy. He claimed that only people living in 'shitty houses' oppose a new data center. Residents were quick to call him out for being disrespectful. This kind of pushback highlights the challenges that come with building new AI infrastructure – it's not just about technology, but also about community acceptance and trust. I mean, who talks about people's homes like that?

On a more positive note, OpenAI has broken ground on a massive 1GW data center project in Michigan as part of its Stargate initiative. This project aims to expand access to AI, create jobs, and support local communities. It's an interesting example of how AI infrastructure can have a direct impact on the economy and daily life. Plus, it's a big deal for Michigan – we're talking about a significant investment in the state's tech future.

Building the infrastructure for the Intelligence Age in Michigan is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: OpenAI breaks ground on a 1GW data center project in Michigan as part of Stargate, building AI infrastructure to expand access, create jobs, and support communities. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
